first_call: ^, status check. Are you inside the facility?
^Yes, I'm in. Met the facility manager

c-0: ^You: I'm inside. Met Robert Chen, the facility manager.
c-1: ^You: Inside the facility. Beginning investigation now.
c-2: ^You: I'm in. Anything new from your end?

b: ^Good. Chen's cooperation will be valuable—he knows those systems inside out.
^Use his SCADA expertise when you need it.
^How's he taking the cover story? Suspicious or cooperative?

c-1: ^You: Skeptical, but he's complying with the audit cover.
c-2: ^You: I told him the truth about ENTROPY. He's fully on board.

chen_cooperation_acknowledged: ^Good. Maintain the cover until you have hard evidence. Then bring him in fully if needed.
chen_skeptical_acknowledged: ^Keep him cooperative. If you find evidence of compromise, that'll convince him.
chen_revealed_acknowledged: ^Bold move. But if he's committed, use him—SCADA expertise will help identify their attack vector.
investigation_update: ^Identify the attack vector. Disable it. Capture operatives if possible—especially Voltage.
intel_update: ^Nothing new. Signals intelligence still shows encrypted traffic between the facility and external nodes.
^Whatever they're planning, it's scheduled for 0800. You've got time, but not much.
first_call_objectives: ^Priority one: find how they're compromising the SCADA network.
^Look for server room access, network infrastructure, anything that explains remote control.

b: ^Chen can point you to the right systems.

first_call_end: ^Stay sharp. These aren't amateurs. If you encounter hostiles, defend yourself.
event_server_room_entered: ^, good work reaching the server room.
^That's their access point—SCADA network infrastructure runs through there.
^I see a VM terminal for network investigation

c-0: ^You: There's a network investigation terminal here. SCADA backup server.

vm_guidance: ^Perfect. Use it to scan the SCADA network topology.
^Identify compromised systems, enumerate services, find their attack mechanism.
^Submit flags at the drop-site terminal when you find intelligence.
investigation_guidance: ^Look for network access points, compromised services, remote control mechanisms.
^They had three operatives here for hours—they installed something.
^Find it, analyze it, and we'll know how to disable their attack.

b: ^—you've already encountered hostiles. Stay alert. There may be more.
^Watch your back. Those operatives are armed and won't hesitate.

event_attack_mechanism_identified: ^, I'm seeing your flag submissions. Outstanding work.
^You've identified their complete attack infrastructure. Three-vector approach: physical bypass devices, SCADA malware, remote trigger.
^All three vectors need to be disabled

c-0: ^You: All three attack vectors need to be neutralized to stop this.

three_vector_confirmation: ^Correct. Physical devices on the dosing stations, malicious SCADA script, and their command laptop.
^Disable all three, and the attack is dead.
trigger_location_discussion: ^Based on your intel, the remote trigger is with Voltage—maintenance wing command center.
^That's where you'll find him. And that's where this ends.
voltage_priority_update: ^He knows about The Architect, multi-cell coordination, future operations.

c-0: ^You: Should I prioritize capturing Voltage even if it's riskier?
c-1: ^You: Understood. I'll attempt to capture him.
c-2: ^You: Attack prevention is priority one. Capture is secondary.

capture_vs_speed_guidance: ^Your call on the ground. Here's the analysis:
^Capture Voltage: High intel value, but riskier engagement. He may threaten to trigger early.
^Prioritize attack disabling: Lower risk, guaranteed mission success, but we lose intelligence.
^I trust your judgment. Choose based on the tactical situation.

c-0: ^You: I'll make the call when I confront him. Tactical situation dependent.

capture_attempt_acknowledged: ^—if he threatens to trigger the attack, stop him by any means.
attack_priority_acknowledged: ^Solid priorities. Stop the attack. If Voltage escapes but the attack fails, that's still a win.
judgment_trusted: ^That's the right approach. Adapt to what you find.
final_phase_briefing: ^One—neutralize Voltage and any remaining operatives in the maintenance wing.
^Two—secure or destroy the remote trigger laptop.
^Three—disable physical bypass devices and SCADA malware.

b: ^You've neutralized one operative. Expect resistance from the others.
^All three operatives are still active. Be ready for combat.

c-0: ^You: Ready. Moving to the maintenance wing now.

b: ^. Textbook operation so far. Finish it.
^Good work so far. Stay sharp for the final push.
^Be careful. This is the most dangerous phase.

final_encouragement: ^240,000 people are counting on you. Bring it home.
player_guidance_request: ^, go ahead. What do you need?

b: ^Get to the server room. That's where they accessed the SCADA network.

^Defeat the operative patrolling chemical storage. They have the master keycard.

combat_help: ^Use cover. These operatives have training, but so do you.
^Stealth takedowns when possible. Direct engagement if necessary.

b: ^Chen might have intel on operative locations if you ask.

vm_challenge_help: ^Start with network scanning—Nmap. Map the SCADA topology.
^Then enumerate services—FTP and HTTP will have intelligence files.
^Finally, exploit vulnerable services to access attack control mechanisms.

b: ^Chen can provide SCADA context if you need technical clarification.

call_final_end: ^Stay safe out there. Call if you need me.
